title: Asarum contractum
taxonomy: Asarum contractum is classified in the following way: Asarum contractum belongs to the family Aristolochiaceae but is often placed in the family Asaraceae, which is also known as the wild ginger family. The classification of Asarum contractum is subject to ongoing research and may change over time.

intro: Asarum contractum, also known as Contracted Birthwort or Small Winter Ginger, is a perennial plant belonging to the Aristolochiaceae family, characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and clusters of small, yellow flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Asarum contractum include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
